channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: WEAT - the play everyone is missing — 58 replies — 232.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
WEAT - the play everyone is missing

Open thread • Replies: 58 • Updated: 2026-03-12 22:36 UTC

OP

This is potentially the most consequential second-order effect from the oil crisis and almost nobody in retail trading is talking about it yet.
Oil and natural gas aren't just fuel — they're the foundational feedstock for nitrogen fertilizer.
The Haber-Bosch process converts natural gas into ammonia, which becomes urea, ammonium nitrate, and other fertilizers.
Roughly 2-3% of global energy consumption goes to fertilizer production alone. When natural gas prices doubled after Russia invaded Ukraine, fertilizer prices tripled and we got a global food price crisis.

Now consider: European natural gas has nearly doubled already in one week. Qatar - one of the world's largest LNG exporters - declared force majeure. 20% of global LNG transits Hormuz. Europe rebuilt its entire energy security strategy around LNG after cutting off Russian gas.

The third-order effect: We're entering spring planting season in the Northern Hemisphere
RIGHT NOW. Farmers make input decisions in March-April. If fertilizer prices spike 50-100% (which is beginning), farmers either plant less, use less fertilizer (lower yields), or absorb costs they can't recover. The food price inflation from this shows up in August-October harvests -
5-6 months from now.

They can release strategic oil reserves. There are no strategic fertilizer reserves. Food prices are about to be fucked, and as you can see from the chart - if you get in early, you can profit. Let retards from Reddit chase oil prices around. Go ahead and wait for them at the finish line.

You’re welcome fuckfaces

Early replies (first 3)

61957829
>>61957787
>This is potentially the most consequential second-orde
stopped reading here. i hate faggots who try to sound smart. judging by your pic, you think wheat is going to pump? youre a fucking retard.

61957846
Ukraine was also a producer of wheat thobeit

61957902
>>61957829
I’m also right. I’m sorry you get all your trading ideas from mouth breathing retards (HURR DURR LETS LONG OIL. HURR DURR LETS BUY HIMS. Wut about muh chainlink!).

Sometimes you might want to try listening to well thought out theories and intelligence for a change. Yes you fucking retard, wheat is going to asplode. But I actually prefer if you stay out; I’d rather someone like you not profit a dime off it

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61963392
>>61963375
Good point I’m curious to see the response

61963749
>>61963375
Over a third of the US corn supply is used to produce ethanol for fuel blends too. But if ethanol production doesn't remain profitable... Wat den?

61963858
>>61963749
Why would that happen though? Ethanol is almost all used to cut gasoline and in a world with higher oil prices more ethanol would be used to cut gas in higher ratios (or at least there would be an incentive to so so where it's possible). The amount of ethanol in fuel does have a government mandated floor, though. Ethanol is too important to farmers for any political party or politician to want to fuck with it in a way that hurts them.

61963915
TLDR
Grow your own taters and become indoor chicken farmer

61963960
>>61963095
>But spring top-dress applications of nitrogen are a separate purchase cycle that’s happening right now, and that’s the part getting disrupted
Do they not just produce fertiliser year round despite the fact that it is only purchased/used at specific times of the year?
I don't know anything about the process so maybe I'm way off base, but my assumption would be that whatever equipment is used to produce it is pretty specialised and would be running year round, and producers or wholesalers of fertiliser would just warehouse the production until it was ready to be shipped to farmers.
If the main application is in Spring then shouldn't 90% of the fertiliser needed to meet demand already be produced?

61963967
I already got heemed trying to play what's going on with this shit. Maybe I'll double down

61969403
>>61963967
Just hold like you say for August

61969507
>>61962395
>Age of Empires taught me more about commodity chains than any econ class
bas'd
now,never copy&paste ai slop into your op ever again, and ew're good

61969526
>>61963095
>There is no substitute at scale.
>full stop
>You can’t just ‘switch’
>yields drop. Not to zero, but meaningfully.
> right now, not when the war ends, not when ships start moving again.
>>61963105
>here’s the key thing —
>You’re not betting on x. You’re betting on y
>—

why even post? why areyou wasting everyones time with llm slop. pathetic fucking NIGGER

61969532
>>61963375
Corn is shit nutrition and can be substituted with onions if it rises in price. Wheat is irreplaceable and literally in the Bible

61971229
>>61957787
okay but why wouldnt one play fertilizer companies directly instead of WEAT? their leftover fertilizer stock is probably worth a nice premium

61971244
>>61957787
Not reading all that gay shit,
Which stock, crypto or commodity and am I long or short?

61971528
>>61969526
damn thanks for pointing that out, I almost ordered some wheat. What a gigantic nigger monkey OP is

61971627
>>61971528
My non crypto portfolio is 20% wheat thanks to OP

61971647
>>61971627
want a medal wheatboy?

61971707
>>61971627
Wheat sounds too autistic not to buy after a beautiful OP. Imagine how ass hurt people would be if you said you got rich off wheat. Peak autism Thanks op

61971748
>>61971647
You may address me as Wheatbaron, nowheater
>>61971707
It won't be Generational wealth, but it's definitely an easy 5x.

61971766
MOS and NTR. Nice comfy leaf fertilizer companies. Lil pump already

61971867
>>61971766
My biggest hold for fertilizer is a nice little company called ASIX. Might pick up some MOS though, elbows up

61972000
i bought wheat and natural gas ETFs (the actual commodities not companies)
rate my financial choice

61972100
>>61957787
I'm buying in, good posts.

61972157
>>61971229
Because they're the ones being choked?
Oh but it's cool, the air they're holding in their lungs is at a premium

61972169
>>61957787
Wheat is stupid, the goyim(cattle) will just switch to other feed like corn.

61972429
>/biz/ confidently falls for 2026 LLM slop
impressive. the future is grim

61973035
>>61972157
yes but, if their current stock is enough for the ongoing season you wont see a rise in wheat price?

61973101
>>61972169
Sneed

61975207
>>61971867
>already up 12% today
Thanks OP, if you didn't post about wheat I'd never of bought this

61976022
>>61971766
Holy shit, nice tickers anon. I got some and am enjoying what looks to be the dual commodity / gamma squeeze going on.

61976029
>>61972429
>redditor refuses to engage in a clearly good trade simply because someone used a obvious high quality LLM to do DD for them.

You should go buy some gamestock anon; I don’t think this trade is for you

61976522
>>61976022
You seem fairly confident in this, can you explain how a retard like myself is meant to profit here? Is it too late?

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 48 replies — 192.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 48 • Updated: 2026-03-12 22:27 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61948998
>>61948977
100$ end of bull run

>>61948983
Wow, these antisemites are self reporting nowadays. Jannies!!! Ban him! He dares to say something bad about Israel. NOT ALLOWED. UNACCEPTABLE !!! He's a NAZI. Jannies, help. We've got a true nazi over here.

61951499
https://polkadothalving.com/ 5 more days

61951573
>>61951499
very good....i hope 3$ in april
75 in 2 year

61951605

61956435
https://polkadothalving.com/ 4 more days

61959501

61960135

61962610

61962631
>>61942667

61962633
>>61962631

61962637
>>61962631
Also, 1 recommendation. Stake on Bifrost or Hydration. Or through the official staking dashboard https://staking.polkadot.cloud/#/overview. Don't use exchanges, they give you peanuts.

61962645

"
$TDOT is the first-ever @Polkadot
ETP in the US, now live and #NasdaqListed.

It’s official.
The first Polkadot U.S. ETF is live and trading on Nasdaq.
"

61962682

DOT IS GOING TO THE MOON

61962686

61962688
>>61962682
>DOT IS GOING TO THE MOON
100$ end of bull run

61962693

61962715

61962726
>>61962715
>i dont sell, i dont care
I DOT buy, I DOT care

61965530
DOT? That thing is put into my brakes?

61966637
back to $1?

61968186

>i don't sell, i don't care

61968274
This crypto is 1.50.
The fuck?

Not a stock!

61971016
https://polkadothalving.com/ 2 more days

61971138
Prepare yourselves guys. A once in a lifetime event is about to happen. The millennial lunar eclipse is about to happen.

61972032
>>61971138
Locked and loaded fellow DOThead

61972844
>>61932834
This is the pedochain and the total ruin of investors. This crap is just going to go lower, all the way to zero.

61975396
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2031796064085242150
"
Ongoing economic upgrade for Polkadot introduces DOT scarcity in 3 days.

The first Polkadot Halving will deliver a daily reduction of issuance by 53%:
• Maximum supply: 2.1B DOT
• Reduced long-term inflation
• A more predictable monetary model
"

I just hope you guys understand that even though the inflation is dropping that this WILL NOT have an immediate effect. Stuff takes time. If you believe that the price will sky rocket after the inflation drop then you're in for a disappointing experience.

61976472
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9jK-NcRmVcw
https://polkadothalving.com/ 1 more day

item.title: THE GIANT IS STARTING TO BLEED — 35 replies — 140.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
THE GIANT IS STARTING TO BLEED

Open thread • Replies: 35 • Updated: 2026-03-12 22:36 UTC

OP

THE GIANT IS STARTING TO BLEED

Early replies (first 3)

61971966
>>61971964
>2022

61971967
>>61971964
What did he mean by this?
Genuine question.

61972001
>>61971967
>What did he mean by this?


>BlackRock (BLK) Stock Plunges 5% as $26B Private Credit Fund Limits Investor Exits
https://blockonomi.com/blackrock-blk-stock-plunges-5-as-26b-private-credit-fund-limits-investor-exits/

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61972012
>>61972006
They aren't letting people withdraw funds from the casino because somewhere their balance and leverages can't cover it.

61972016
>>61971967
DEI and managed decline. All the stuff /pol/ says is happening that isn't happening but it is a good thing if it does.

61972021
>>61971967
Make everybody brown, gay, and retarded, because this is the only jews can dominate

61972023
https://youtu.be/KwwN5kwjAtQ

61972040
>>61972012
What’s that mean for us?

61972045
>>61972040
No refunds

61972051
>>61972040
You'll be bailing them out when they steal your retirement to make the frogs gay and the girl bosses empowered.

61972054
>>61972040
Nothing. The fund in question invests in illiquid assets and has a contractual withdrawal rate limit of 5% per quarter. Some investor tried to withdraw 9%, got told no, and went to the press crying about it and trying to make a stink.

61972057
>>61972001
>25 Billion in garbage credit
>13 Trillion dollar in assets under management

Cry me a river goyim

61972072
>>61972054
Is that what all that "Blackrock restricts investors' withdrawals¡!!1!" was about? It's always some stupid shit like that.

61972075
>>61972045
>>61972051
>>61972054

What does that mean for chainlink holders

61972077
>>61971966
Yeah, I was going to say this behaviour forcing has been happening for years now, it’s why modern video games have fat niggers in them and all suck

61972086
>>61972075
1k eoy

61972090
>oil

61972097
>>61972023
I hope Iran bombs the living shit out Israel.

61972701
>>61971964
To be desu, i disconnected.from.the real world for a while...

61972705
>>61972097
Would be nice of them!

61972730
>>61971964
They're doing so well with this idea that they won't let their investors access their funds lol. 5% hahahaha wtf.

61972794
>>61972016
Niggers on every imaginable piece of media. Kikes run every institutional narrative. Goy companies tell workers to be less white. Women fuck niggers to prove themselves they're good persons then become single mothers with black eyes. Rapefugees raping Europe out of existence. Her penis.
>isn't happening
STFU kike.

61972812
>>61971964
>>61971967
>we have to force behaviors

61972833
>>61972040
Cash out your 401k and buy gold.

61972926
>>61971967
it means he owns your ass

61973676
>>61971967
you guys still dont get that the DEI shit was invented by blackrock so they can force other companies to hire their consultants?

61973694
>>61973676
This.

61973819
>>61971964
Shoving DEI down everyone’s throat by incentivizing it on the backend. Make shitty DEI games, movies, TV and other cultural slip that won’t sell but Blackrock will pay you for it. That is Faggot Finks way of forcing behavior. Staking that Kike to an Anthill would be magnificent.

61973844
>>61971967
You will never sell and you WILL be happy

61974386
I guess this is what they mean with ID verification being enforced in almost every country. Remember he's also the chairman of WEF now.

61976455
>>61974386
Yeah you will be debanked for reputational risk if you aren't a gay race communist

61976497
>>61973676
It was actually invented by auditing companies, most in Europe, who saw American millennials pissing and shitting themselves in occupy wallstreet, and in turn decided to gave their euro millennial interns kart blanche to begin shitting things up.
They then created the consulting grift industry, and yes it was then also picked up by firms like blackrock and gamified to the point of parody.

61976523
>>61972097
I hope they manage to kill 10% of US population.
that'd be swell.

item.title: Thoughts on these business ideas? I asked Deepseek the highest ROI businesses to do. Not sure I'd waste my time if I tri… — 10 replies — 29.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Thoughts on these business ideas? I asked Deepseek the highest ROI businesses to do. Not sure I'd waste my time if I tri…

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-03-12 22:17 UTC

OP

Thoughts on these business ideas? I asked Deepseek the highest ROI businesses to do. Not sure I'd waste my time if I tried this though. I haven't heard of anyone else doing these.

Early replies (first 3)

61975699
Dude if you ask AI to pick some crypto for you it will answer with:
>BTC
>ETH
>Cardano (lol)
>XRP
>Link
all bottom of the barrel shit crypto that will make you no money in this life unless you are already rich in which case I guess putting a huge bag on BTC wouldn't be wrong

AI has no insight it aggregates the average data, so you just got the average business idea from midwits on reddit for the current timeline, no one pays for that stuff and no one serious builds that stuff
You are literally much better off using your monkey brain and think of something

61975726
>>61975699
Yeah, AI is pretty bad for predicting anything. I was just curious how far off it was with these suggestions and if anyone would offer better ideas. I was hoping it would give an edge, anything to even the playing field given the shit economy and housing market.

61975829
>>61975625
#1 sounds good. 2 and 3, I wouldn’t bother.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61975863
>>61975625
1 sounds good if you are a member of reddit, if I open a small business today I literally get swarmed with invoicing/crm/bullshit sw made from actual engineers from actual companies that have actual tech support for as low as 5/10$ per month or something retarded like that.
No I don't want to pay the same money to some anon scumbag that "solves" problems by feeding them to a shitty AI

61975894
>>61975863
Can you recommend anything better? I'm willing to work on anything, just need something good to focus on beyond my current engineer job, which is kind of a dead end gig given that most people are in their 40s-50s at the company and I'm still just 29.

61975909
>>61975863
> pay a boomer company an overpriced monthly fee for your niche SaaS software
> or hire an ai bro to build a perfect clone of it for a one time fee
Gee I wonder which is the better idea here

61975987
>>61975894
told you, use your brain
find literally anything that pays money download real world data and run the numbers. Run the numbers until your preconceptions melt away and you are able to see the real constraints. After a bit you should find an angle that is fresh and underplayed (not hard since most business run on autopilot) that's your play.
>BUUUUUUUT what specifically?
fuck if I know, how about transporting refrigerated medicine + supermarket supplies?
There is people that make a living doing that, why can't you
>BOOOOORING
ye everything is boring at the start. You start liking it when you have more success than others and money comes in

61975993
>deepseek

unc moment

61976126
>>61975987
Thanks, yeah I guess the tricky thing is finding what can scale well and isn't impossible to do while working a full time job already. Consulting or something online I can do when I'm still on the clock.

>>61975993
what's currently better? I thought it was still as good as chatgpt's paid version if you toggle Deepthink

61976444
>>61975625
there are more than a few companies selling these ai services, why would a business take the product of joe nobody instead of theirs

item.title: Why haven't you taken the passport bro/foreign wife pill? — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why haven't you taken the passport bro/foreign wife pill?

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-03-12 22:34 UTC

OP

Why haven't you taken the passport bro/foreign wife pill?

Just did my taxes MFJ and I got like an $8k refund, normally I pay.

Plus they are cuter and less bitchy than their western counterparts.

Early replies (first 3)

61976375
>>61976281
im tempted for the easymode but cba living in SEA

61976386
I want a cute, loving Nigerian bf who will make me jollof rice, bros...

61976398
>>61976281
>Why haven't you taken the passport bro/foreign wife pill?
I have dignity

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61976417
>>61976386
i'm nigerian but i don't know if i'm "cute"
i'll learn to suck dick and get really /fit/ for you if you get me out of this hellhole tho

61976456
>>61976398
Luka Doncic just got divorced so, if that dignity I don't want it.

61976471
>>61976417
How tall are you?

61976509
1. I don't want to get scammed.
2. If I wanted a Flip wife I could easily find a nurse here but I've dated a Filipina before and yeah they're alright people but they're also culturally Mormons and don't really value learning for its own sake. They make god-tier wives I'm sure but they behave more like your nurse with the addition of being super-clingy and kinda nuts, also you can expect to be sending 10% of your money back to her family.
3. Visa fees and the general process for citizenship are no joke. This is on top of catalogue wives not really being able to earn much money or deal with a new society (if you don't want to live in some Africa-tier hell).

I've thought about it a lot over the years but I'm also very aware that half the reason I'm not married is my own standards. If I do end up biting the bullet on this I'd go to Romania/Moldova.

niggers tongue my anus

item.title: Today someone said he makes $125k a year and then another person said >what do you do to make $125k >that is a lot >are… — 225 replies — 199.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Today someone said he makes $125k a year and then another person said >what do you do to make $125k >that is a lot >are…

Open thread • Replies: 225 • Updated: 2026-03-12 21:29 UTC

OP

Today someone said he makes $125k a year and then another person said
>what do you do to make $125k
>that is a lot
>are you in LA?
>why are you lying?
Then I said
>150k is middle class
>no offense, when others don’t believe others, it means they are poor or make less
Then he replied
>150k is not middle class
>60k-80k is middle class
I said
>$125k cannot afford a middle class lifestyle and most jobs paying $125k are in big cities and there is high cost of living
Then he starts lying and saying he is 13
The conversation is just him seething someone makes $125k and arguing that 150k is not middle class.
I tell him due to increased cost of living and wealth gap, even 1m is nothing and 10% of 50% of the consumption and 90% are not doing well.
Why are poor people always seething?
$125k isn’t even middle class. How poor do you guys that guy is? 40k? 70k?
He was really defensive about $150k being middle class.
Do poor people think they are middle class?
This isn’t the first time this has happened.

Early replies (first 3)

61965995
>>61965964
Middle class is self sufficient, it requires an income that can support a household, retirement, children, education(including college), stay at home wife (or) nanny, 2+ modern cars, insurance, 1-3 vacations/yr . I would say 125k-250k depending on area. Lower class normies think they are middle class, middle class is mostly dead. Upper class has excess income after meeting those needs. Lower class requires gov assistance(welfare, healthcare, public school, etc).

61966005
>>61965964
>>$125k cannot afford a middle class lifestyle and most jobs paying $125k are in big cities and there is high cost of living
When people ssay middle class they mean they've got a wife and together they make about 140k or something and can easily afford a suburban house in areas within 2 hours of major cities, especially in southern and midwestern cities like Omaha and Dallas. Your idea of middle class is jetsetting around and banging sloots whenever you want.

61966016
>>61965995
> 1-3 vacations/yr .
1 vacation, maybe dude.
3? Lmao.
I think middle class lifestyle now requires a lot higher income than what people think now.
That wasn’t the first time someone got really defensive with “no, 60-80k is middle class.”
Fucking annoying but good conversation I guess. The ignorance is annoying.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61970642
>>61965964
The middle class are just rich people with poor financial sense.
They finance like three vehicles, constantly buy new yard toys(mowers, blowers, fourwheelers, etc.) and stay in eternal debt slavery to up the debt slave score.

61970911
>>61967708
at least its 4c content and not reposts from twitter

61971594
>>61965964
Most Americans want to lump themselves in middle class when in reality they are working class people in denial. Marx might've had a lot of retarded ideas but his definitions of class make a lot more sense then vaguely defining lifestyles and their relation to income.

Lumpenproles are nigs, broke NEETS, druggies and other welfare queens, proles are the majority of people who absolutely must labor in order to maintain their living standards long term, petit bourgeoisie have either enough capital that they can live on the profits generated or have a highly valuable skill set that one can largely dictate the course of their labor, but still must ultimately labor, and the bourgeoisie who have fuck you levels of money & power.

61972335
>>61970586
Lmao mad

61972470
Nigger

61972520
>>61970586
KWAB

61972535
>>61971594
> Most Americans want to lump themselves in middle class when in reality they are working class people in denial
Middle class is working class though. The people making 60k only argued that they are middle class. Being working class or not is never mentioned.

61972540
>>61967215
They live low class lives full of comforts. Better than peasants 100s years ago who has to farm and toil.

61972601
>>61971594
>bourgeoisie who have fuck you levels of money & power.

More like lazy parasites who were born into lazy larasitic bloodlines that control the government.

That's the highest level of wealth.
All should aim to be a lazy parasite.
The only way to do that is to become a deranged sex addict. Then money will flow 24/7 and money ideas etc.

>>61972540
true.

61972856
>>61972601
>All should aim to be a lazy parasite.
Kill yourself kike

61972864
>>61972856
>Kill yourself kike
I voted for this

61973005
>>61965995
By that definition I'm upper class. But doesn't feel like it.

61974567
>>61965964
I love reading these mercantilist disputes between Ameripoors.
>$150k is middle class!!!1!!1
>no you fucking subhuman nigger, for $150k you cannot even afford an apartment in a big city
>it's fault of jews/trump/biden/obama/mexicans
>$300k is middle class, not $150k!!!!!11!

What really matters, is how much you can save. There's a person who earns $50k but lives with his parents and saves up $40k yearly. And there is a person who earns $100k but throws out his money on everything and barely saves up $10k. And who's the winner here? Work smart, not hard.

61974600
>>61974567
Not really. Don’t give me that bullshit about someone saving 1k is more well off than someone with 150k income.
Dumb ass.
>>61973005
>excess income
You are supposed to afford those things comfortably.

61974633
>>61974567
> What really matters, is how much you can save. There's a person who earns $50k but lives with his parents and saves up $40k yearly. And there is a person who earns $100k but throws out his money on everything and barely saves up $10k. And who's the winner here? Work smart, not hard.
You’re the retarded earning 50k huh lol

61974733
>>61974600
I'm doing it quite comfortably. I have next to no expenses. I save around 80% of my income.

61974774
>>61974733
You missed the point of the op.
Making 150k vs 50k. Ignoring other dumb shit idiot anons like you pull out your ass.

61974793
>>61974774
I'm not gonna say how much I earn, but you are right, I'm a dumb retard. GN anon.

61975034
>>61974793
I don’t need to know anything specific bro.
If you need a job, then its usually less than 400k.

61975068
>>61975034
Close enough, consulting.

61975234
>>61975068
Yeah. Most jobs cap out. Not sure why you were so defensive. My main topic was 50k and 150k.
I was talking to some consulting guy in nyc and I told him I used to make 200k and now retired. And he got so angry and triggered.
>you do NOT make 200k!!!!!
And he said he works for high networth people. But if he did, why was 200k so surprising
(it is because most income caps out and out of the general population, 200k is rarer).
Thing is, most rich people know whats possible so to see him so defensive, he was probably busting his ass for his work.
One thing I learned is well off people always believe others.
Poor people almost always immediately reply with
>no you dont
>yeah and im elon musk
>ur lying

61975263
"Class" is a Marxist term designed to make you hate people richer than yourself.

The terminology falls apart with any examination. For example, a doctor making $500K per year is "working class" because he works, but is in the top 1% of incomes.

If you want to communicate properly, just say high income, middle income, low income, etc.

61975270
>>61975263
A doctor IS working class.
There is working class and capitalist class.

61975379
>>61975270
But a person earning $30K a year can put $3k into a dividend stock and start collecting "surplus value" which Marxists decry as capitalist theft. Is he a working class or capitalist class? What about a worker who slowly invests over 40 years until his dividends pay more than his wage, but he's still working?
The terminology fails hard to communicate anything useful.

61975502
>>61965995
If you can afford a nanny and three vacations a year you are upper class full stop. Most normies get two weeks a year tops, with some uncs capping out at four.

61975528
>>61975379
Posts like yours dont really promote discussion and just shit flinging.
You KNOW what capitalist class and working class is. Why you talking nonsense?
> What about a worker who slowly invests over 40 years until his dividends pay more than his wage, but he's still working?
Obviously after that, then you can start talking about the lifestyle his dividends can bring in.

61975585
>>61975379
Marxists are ugly losers.

61975620
>>61975502
3 vacations. Yeah. Like over a month vacation kek. Normal bottom 90% or even 95% cannot afford this.
And they face high gas prices, air tickets price increases and food inflation due to Iran War lmaoo

61976191
>>61965964
60-80k was middle class before covid. Now it's working poor.

61976253
>>61975263
Retard, class comes from ancient Rome.

item.title: Chainlink explicitly mentioned by SWIFT — 104 replies — 115.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chainlink explicitly mentioned by SWIFT

Open thread • Replies: 104 • Updated: 2026-03-12 21:43 UTC

OP

You may all sperg the fuck out now. Never fucking selling.

Early replies (first 3)

61964214
>ZOG Swift intern casually poops LLM-generated report
>CHAINCHINK IS BOOMING BROS
actually delusional. $9.

61964222
don't care don't care
price price price
you lost the game, stinkies
PERIOD
stop trying to reclaim some shred of lost dignity. just take the heckin' L

61964252
>>61964222
i dont really give a fuck about link but this image disgusts me

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61968895
>>61968358
>CL employees have officially stated that there is no IPO happening

posts like these are great. they truly show how incredibly naive and inexperienced a link boyscout is

>duuuuuuude the janitor at the building i work at said the owner isn't selling

as if any of these conversations would involve a 26 yr old trans "god" who's job it is to copy and paste buzzwords on x

61968914
>>61968875
didnt read your slop lol Whatever you wrote still doesnt justify the XRP spammers being clinically retarded

61968946
>>61968616
>how the fuck does swift even make money

swift's purpose is not to make money. it's a co-op

they do collect some annual fees from the member banks to facilitate operations. they are not aiming to generate a profit, and they aren't taking a cut of cross border transactions if that's what you're thinking


not for nothing bro but you could've had answer to your q in like 15 seconds w ai search

61968951
>>61968914
>Whatever you wrote still doesnt justify the XRP spammers being clinically retarded

the funny part is you are the exact same as them

in your head your ego is telling you that you're a secret genius while the xrp cult just doesn't "get it". but the reality is they just went 7x last year while you made nothing

your ego is saving you from the brutal reality

61969258
>>61968951
so which one are you

61969267
>literal retard brown mentally ill gooners are making more money than you
feels bad man
but pain is temporary, glory is eternal! WAGMI MARINES

61969348
>>61964199
It's amazing the lack of interest in LINK. It's get the best news and doesn't move at all. In 2019 this news would have pumped it to $100.

61969656
>>61968951
>the funny part is you are the exact same as them

Chainlink was mentioned, XRP wasnt, end of story. Token price doesnt matter here. Youre hung up on it only because it’s the only way you can downplay the fact that Chainlink got mentioned while XRP didnt lol. And just because link didn’t pump doesnt change the fact that Swift mentioned Chainlink.

61969683
>>61969656
>Chainlink was mentioned, XRP wasnt, end of story
yup

61969746
>>61965107
Take your delusional ramblings back to /pol/ you fuckin freak

61969776
>>61967953
no, it's a guy with severe mental illness who has been posting the same image occasionally in link threads for many years. he's probably been extremely blown the fuck out on whatever else he put his money into and can only get satisfaction by making fun of stinkies, because he didn't buyed when it was below a dollar

61969981
>>61969656
>Chainlink was mentioned, XRP wasnt, end of story.

and? bro you're cheering on an offshore company that you own zero equity in just like a sportsball fan

then you had to project me as some big xrp holder. i fucking wish. i actually bought the line that it was dead and didn't buy any this cycle

they used to say to always inverse biz, i should've listened

61969992
>>61969683
>>Chainlink was mentioned, XRP wasnt, end of story
>yup

you meant to say chainlink labs i believe


this doesn't help the funding token tho kid. you guys keep trying these marketing strategies that haven't worked for 5 years straight.

i take it you've never read what einstein had to say on repeating the same thing over and over

61970155
>>61969981
>and?
I didnt read anything you wrote except the first word.

61970161
>>61964199
>but chainlink was MENTIONED in the swift files

61970974
>>61965559
I don't understand how this fucking chat works. Someone spoonfeed me

61971436
>>61964199
>Price feeds that do zero for token price
>ONLY 20 YEARS TIL PRELIMINARY PRODUCT ROLLOUT!!!! (no exaggeration exactly what SWIFT said)

61972300
>>61968875
>>61968914
>>61970155
Since the Trump pump.

61972330
Cainlink psychosis episode 5789.
Yeah, if there’s ever another huge crypto hype cycle, then ‘muh Chainlink actually getting mentioned somewhere in something serious’ might trigger a massive hype wave and your coin might go to the moon - and hopefully you’ll cash out fast enough.
But honestly, this is getting very cult-like rn.

61972716
>>61972330
>getting
>rn
How new ARE you

61972800
It's over bros, LINK is not new tech, a hidden gem, or a coming 10-100x. LINK is mainstream and is in Twitter roasties bios. It’s a corporate entity now, so Sergey has to dump another 750k LINK to hire talent managers and diversity officers. LINK hasn’t been the biz token for over 5 years now. Just sell anons... i-i-iit’s over. It was a good ride marines.

61972902
>>61965107
>painting yourself blue
Dont threaten me with a good time
>cannibalism
Pure projection; Canninite-baalism you just tattled on yourself watch out for that bro.

61972933
>>61964199
these faggots have been doing trials for as long as i have been on /biz/ lmao. seriously, like 5 years...what the fuck are they doing?

61972954
>>61972933
DUDE JUST ANOTHER TRIAL I PROMISE THIS YEAR IS THE YEAR!!!!!!!!!!

61973267
Pull out of Chainlink while you can, before society portrays it as a Racist currency.

61973778
>>61972933
They are trialing and erroring

61975163
>>61970155
>I didnt read anything you wrote except the first word.

thanks for the update my boy

is my dry cleaning ready yet?

>>61972300

ok. are you saying i should buy some?

61975171
>>61972933
>these faggots have been doing trials for as long as i have been on /biz/

seriously lol
i still remember hearing about trials almost a decade ago

these fuckers run more trials than pfizer lmao

61975886
>>61975171
>10 peels by this ID

61976322
>>61964199
...wait did the mastercard ceo really say swift might not exist in 5 years (a few years ago)?
probably nothing right?

item.title: WFH CHADS REPORT IN — 96 replies — 91.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
WFH CHADS REPORT IN

Open thread • Replies: 96 • Updated: 2026-03-12 21:34 UTC

OP

Let's all laugh at the wagies who have to pay insane amounts for gas to get to work kek

Early replies (first 3)

61953886
>>61953855
Imagine spending half your days pay worth of money just to get to work and back lmao

61954053
>>61953855
antisemitic wfh antizog antimiga chads will inherit the earth

61954077
>>61953886
>half
You mean full for minimum wage chads, don’t forget about rent and bills too.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61963700
>>61963454
Yea I snagged a WFH bank job doing cybersecurity end of 2025. Technically we all work at the office but managers tell their teams where to work and my boomer manager is lazy and won't go to the office basically ever so neither does anyone else on his team.

61963769
Anyone else ride an e-bike?

61964574
I have been WFH for two years. For a long time I was playing tons of video games and working maybe 5 hours per week, kek. Now there's a lot more work to do though, but I still only work maybe 20 hours per week on average. The first half of every day I dedicate to reading news, drinking coffee and shitting. I actually feel bad about it as I want to do meaningful work but since we live in clown world I don't care. I was actually the highest performer by far in previous shit jobs like callcenters.

61965044
>>61954585
Came here to post this.

61965065
>>61954976
I just hired someone (WFH) from LinkedIn, I made a post asking for particular skills and he saw it and had a job the next week.
All social media is cancer but LinkedIn does improve work prospects unfortunately.

61965110
>>61964574
I've been WFH for seven years now, a non-coding job watching over a SAAS application. Most days I just fuck around but for example this week I have lots of work. So I fucked around all morning, did nothing during work hours, but now I am bored so I am gonna put in around 4 hours of real work until I feel sleepy. That's probably going to carry me for a couple days.

The flexibility is great its basically like being a full paid NEET if you have a minimum of willpower to actually strategically do work when absolutely needed. I actually get great ratings and raises every year

61965143
WFH healthcare Epic guy here. Have an offer to consult for big bucks, but it will require I leave my home periodically to travel. What do?

61965186
>>61965143
dinners with american boomers is the absolutely most painful part of my WFH job but it hasn't happened in over a year now. I'd say its worth it

61965203
>>61965186
Leaving a full time position for a short contract for the first time scares me, but when you're good (I am) the work keeps coming. Think im going for it.

61965295
Neet reporting in!
0 work per week!

61965410
>>61965110
Very comfy and based anon.

61965438
>>61958532
Are you me, lmao. I scroll through a lot of maps when I'm bored.

61965873
>>61954356
Holy comfy wtf
How much do you get paid and what's your job?

61965895
>>61958588
>>61959006
Kino, I have a mild secret sex fetish

61966734
>>61953855
>Let's all laugh at the wagies who..
Silly wagie, don't act like you're better than the rest of the cucks. If you're a wagie in any shape or form you are a lesser lifeform compared by default, to the NEET elite. Wagens truly say the darndest things.

61966978
>>61965203
Contract came in, no travel after all

61967069
>>61953855
Anyone else have a low 6 figure wfh job but hate it? Been at my place 4 years and worked my way up. Have a 2 year old and expecting another one. Not leaving it anytime soon but it’s starting to drain my soul. It’s comfy but has busy seasons and they blow. Anyone else feeling the golden cuffs?>>61953855

61967221
>>61959314
>Be careful
>When you are literally fulfilling your genetic perogative
No thanks, i will impregnate my wife on the clock as much as possible.

61967504
I work 2 jobs, one is full remote, but the other is 4 days in office, one from home. I've been actively looking to replace that one with a second full remote job.

61968042
>>61965895
>having penis in vagina sex with your wife is fetish material
Do zoomies really?

61968154
>>61954162
i just do the this when i'm not in the mood for work

61969091
>>61968042
It is if you're doing it in secret while on a call. Fetishes aren't always physical. I admit the lunch break one really isn't.

61969156
>>61969091
Just a variant of exhibitionism which, yes, I am into. Nothing like sex outdoors where you can get caught.

61969187
>>61969156
>exhibitionism
Fuck this is what I was trying to think of. Thanks

61969207
Goddamit I'm looking for a full WFM job, but it's really difficult these days, in my country the standard is 3 days in the office.

61969766
>>61968154
I guess your company must have non-existent monitoring software.

61970946
I checked and I was in office 25 times in 2025. The job is officially billed as hybrid, expecting to be in office 1-2 times per week. But everyone up to the sr director is constantly looking for ways to not come in lol

61973360
Another great thing about WFH is the ability to monitor world news, the stock market and make trades, earning even more money during paid hours.

61976039
>>61969766
Anyway to check for these?
I'd honestly doubt most companies even bother because if you're doing your work it shouldn't matter.
Regardless, I don't do shit at home except the bare minimum and I just started working there, I'll see if they say anything to me.

61976274
>>61976039
No idea how to check desu, maybe in task manager but you'd have to know what to look for. All I know is that mouseclickers get detected easily. Since you haven't been caught they might not be surveilling. Personally I stay in front of the PC and periodically browse around a lot in manuals, spreadsheets, etc so make it seem like I'm working activity-wise in case there is some kind of software activity report on employees. Meanwhile I am doing stuff on my phone or other computer.

item.title: there is absolutely no way all this CAPEX spending on AI will generate the huge amounts of profits to justify costs in a… — 39 replies — 38.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
there is absolutely no way all this CAPEX spending on AI will generate the huge amounts of profits to justify costs in a…

Open thread • Replies: 39 • Updated: 2026-03-12 21:37 UTC

OP

there is absolutely no way all this CAPEX spending on AI will generate the huge amounts of profits to justify costs in any reasonable time frame. Investors are cowards and will run to save their money at the first sign of a slowdown, be it liquidity crunch, growth slowdown, adoption issues, anything. There is a massive over-expectation for AI profitability.

On March 10th 2000, the NASDAQ peaked, having risen 86% in 1999 alone. By March 15th the NASDAQ was down 9.2%. A month later, on April 12th it closed down 25%, ultimately leading to over 50% down by December. The selling in March began very rapidly without a particular cause, but once it gained steam it did not stop. Pets.com was not making enough money and could not possibly generate enough profit within a human lifetime to justify its valuation. Palm Inc had its IPO days before the peak, one of the largest at that point, peaking at $95 a share and crashing down to $6.50 by June of ‘01. Today the company does not exist. Cisco Systems more or less played the role of Nvidia, and only returned to its peak of 2000 this past December, jumping on yet another hype train. Apple had a single day drop of -50% when it missed an earnings report on 9/29/2000.

Why does this matter? The Dotcom bubble has two very key features that it shares with our proposed AI bubble. Insane P/E ratios and unrealistic time frames for return on money invested (ROI). P/E ratios aren’t the best indicators of whether a stock will go up or down, and many analysts casually refer to over-valuations, though only after the fact, when the euphoria of endless growth wears off. It’s a matter of human psychology; once enough investors realize the gravy train is not “guaranteed” or expected fairly soon, they stampede to bonds. No one wants to be a bagholder.

Early replies (first 3)

61970757
I’m not suggesting that MAG7 companies will go under or that the economy will collapse. I am saying that there will need to be a deep correction in the interim period from now until whenever all these data centers come online. More on time frames later. If they don’t figure out how to make it profitable by then, that would just be really funny. The MAG7’s heavy reliance on AI and its own over representation on the SPY/SPX means that if there is a drop, all the average person will see is that AI is nuking their retirement portfolios. The success of AI is holding the American economy hostage, at gun point. More stock market value is dependent on AI’s success than it was on the internet during the .com bubble, plenty more GDP growth was contributed by old economy growth back then. Most of the boom of the market recently has been thanks to debt-based AI spending. Boomer companies like Caterpillar and Generac, are booming because of data center build out contracts.

Let’s look at some P/E ratios. Anything over 20x is typically considered over valued.


S&P 500: 29x (average is typically 19)

NASDAQ: 33x (20yr average is 22)

MAG7

Microsoft/MSFT: 25x
Alphabet/GOOG: 28x
Meta/META: 22x
Amazon/AMZN:30x
Tesla/TSLA: 373x
Apple/APPL: 33x (Apple isn’t nearly as deep in AI as the others)
Nvidia/NVDA: 37x (these guys are killing it thanks to all the debt spending, won’t be pretty if it slows down)

Tech/AI Related

Nebius/NBIS: 587x
Coreweave/CRWV: -334x (forward P/E)
Vertiv/VRT: 73x
Teradyne/TER: 87x
Broadcom/AVGO: 66x
Datadog/DDOG: 394x (companies similar to DDOG are all insanely overvalued)
Lumentum/LITE: 187x
BE Semiconductor/BESIY: 112x
BBAI/AI (companies like this will just fall through the floor; no earnings)

Most companies will likely survive a humbling. Though of the smaller companies are dependent on continuous subsidies, just like they were back during .com. If liquidity taps out, they go under.

61970764
Sam Altman and the rest of the AI cult have done a fantastic job of selling hype, many people are genuinely convinced that AI is taking over and an endless stream of money needs to be burned to build it out. AGI goalposts keep getting moved around.


https://www.windowscentral.com/artificial-intelligence/openai-ceo-sam-altman-claims-agi-might-have-already-whooshed-by

https://arstechnica.com/information-technology/2025/01/sam-altman-says-we-are-now-confident-we-know-how-to-build-agi/

https://www.inthacity.com/blog/tech/will-we-see-agi-by-2028-sam-altman-timeline/


Even for menial or repetitive tasks, hallucinations and errors are extremely commonplace. Sure, it is a new technology that needs refinement, though keep in mind, there is currently no proposed technical solution for hallucination. Considering how much money is being spent on it, AI needs to work incredibly well to generate the profits necessary to recoup spending. It absolutely has its uses, but with all the layoffs happening I swear most of them are just off-shoring. It sounds better on paper and maybe companies are hoping AI can help fill in the gaps. There is no way the tens of thousands or more jobs that have been cut as a result of AI are not mostly cost cutting or off-shoring.

61970770
We all know OpenAI lives on handouts and borrowed time. They already preemptively asked the government for a bailout before even truly needing one. Altman is some sort of modern alchemist, his company is not even remotely close to generating a profit and he keeps getting hundreds of billions in funding. I have to respect the guy. I keep bringing up OpenAI since they are planning one of the largest IPOs in human history. Companies like Google, Microsoft, Meta, etc, are able to fund the loans they take out thanks to their other ventures. How long until they begin to actually generate a profit on all these data centers? Real profit from real customers too, not just the circular economy most of us are familiar with. Did anyone else see Satya Nadella complaining that people are not adopting AI fast enough at Davos? (https://www.techspot.com/news/111012-satya-nadella-warns-ai-must-go-mainstream-avoid.html) How are chatbots and LLMs going to make hundreds of billions of dollars and when? Don’t forget, most tokens are currently very heavily subsidized by these companies to facilitate user adoption. Wonder how much they would have to charge without them, as well as enough to make a profit….

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61971153
I would imagine long term high oil prices would pop the AI bubble because it uses so much fucking energy

61971245
>>61970789
I definitely subscribe to the ethos of the post. But this link you shared (https://www.theneuron.ai/explainer-articles/openai-hits-20b-in-revenue-with-a-17b-problem/) is legitimately an AI puff piece that is written by an LLM. You'll notice that it confuses where a certain comment ("The Hacker News thread was even more blunt. One comment summed it up: "Most vibe coders are building solutions to problems they have. Not everybody is wanting to push solutions to the market. I built 30+ apps for personal use. Never plan to publish.") is coming from.

It thinks the comment is from HackerNews but it is from the linked Reddit thread.

61971307
>>61970731
Government contracts. It is a manhattan project in search of a war

61971653
>>61970731
These companies aren't intended to ever make profit. They are there to develop and be the control grid for the Great Reset, and will crunch and process the massive amount of data for carbon credits, movement lockdowns, social credit scores, and correlate all of it into 'Is X a good citizen or a threat to the state?'

I'm pretty sure all the investors will be rugged.

61971787
>>61970773
>Liquidity is essential for debt based expansion. Every time it dries up, things get ugly.
This. As Michael Howell, foremost expert on liquidity, says, liquidity needs debt (as collateral), and debt needs liquidity (for refinancing). Most debt in today’s capital markets is simply refinancing—no one is repaying their debt.

Howell’s models say that this is a very normal cycle, and incremental liquidity—not absolute liquidity—is drying up, meaning that asset prices are going to be squeezed. Howell’s models put the US markets in the speculation phase, meaning that commodities and bond duration are decent plays. People should be exiting technology, entering energy, and edging in bond duration. During the turbulence phase, long-term Treasuries (TLT) should do well. We are already seeing a lot of credit issues in the market.

61971859
>>61970801
>The logistics for powering all this, even if everything else that was mentioned went over smoothly, are potentially a decade away. Just to really send this point home, the power necessary to actually run all of this is currently non-existent and can, best case scenario, take YEARS to get AI to the peak production capacity, where it is supposed to begin to be powerful enough to run the economy and make record profits.

>>61971307
>Government contracts. It is a manhattan project in search of a war

While I do think the AI companies will take a hit in terms of their stock price (see my prior post >>61971787) due to normal liquidity contraction, I really do not think the logistics are as much of an issue as you seem to believe because, as 34GiEKn/ points out, this is the Manhattan Project of the 21st century.

Do you really think the US is going to just let China, its geostrategic rival/enemy, beat it to probably the most consequential technology ever? Trump is talking about increasing the military budget to $1.5 trillion. When the next financial crisis comes, they aren't going to provide liquidity to the market; they will direct it to the real economy (as Howell shows: Treasury QE > Fed QE). They will run the economy unbelievably hot, approve things left and right (fuck environmental review, fuck NIMBY, fuck anything & everything if National Security is on the line), conduct yield curve control (YCC), and understate inflation so that: (1) the true net present value of obligations for Social Security, Medicare, and Medicaid will be eroded away; (2) weaken the dollar to make reshoring possible.

Old people not prepared will go from eating steaks to eating kibble. But what the fuck are they going to do about it? The old fucks are old, so they ain't starting no revolution.

This technology is not WeWork, and bringing AGI before China will be a whole-of-nation approach, using all levers of statecraft—political, economic, and militaristic.

61971890
Yeah that all makes a little sense I guess, but have you considered that all money is fake and the world is just one giant jew daycare and the nvidia cards and datacenters will be used to make sure youre the best goy you can be.

61972056
>>61971153
Natural Gas does 50% of the American grid right? Hadn't considered that but it's gonna make keeping this crap open way more painful.

>>61971245
I apologize for that, some of the links were solely for their headlines, since most people won't read them but I'm glad you pointed it out.

61972265
>>61971859
I did mention red tape could be pulled.
I don't think AI or AGI is going to live up to the hype and that this civilizational race isn't as necessary as you've described. Whether or not we do it is besides the point, I don't think current tech or chips will get us to AGI.

61973654
Openclaw is already AGI
Cope and seethe

61974409
since the beginning of the AI hype absolutely no task in my office has been replaced by AI computing.

It is always this shady "ROI is diffcult to determine, since it assists with productivity"

61974457
>>61974409
Skill issue
https://x.com/itsolelehmann/status/2031308486815133905

61974468
>>61970731
TLDR

I respect Elon's ambition.

61974779
>>61974457
marketing is not work

61974806
>falling for the space/mars crap in 2026
this board is really filled with moldavian pajeets

61975152
Does anyone have any insight on the discrepancies between user's payments for using AI products and the actual cost to run those products.
Of course a lot of people use some sort of LLM and don't pay anything for it, but often even people who pay for usage are actually spending more in tokens, than it would actually be covered by how much they pay.
Like what is the plan here from this companies, try and get people so hooked on their products that they won't be able to imagine life without them and at some point significantly increase the prices and hope everyone is willing to pay them? Or is there some bullshittery going on here and companies inflate the token costs to run their agents, so that consumers would feel, what a great deal they are getting, when they pay for their products?

61975352
>>61974806
I doubt even moldovans would fall for it, though I'm not sure what that has to do with anything.

>>61975152
10-Ks are extremely opaque on these topics, they're all packaged with other products and not broken down at all.

It's a good question though, LLM tokens are currently very subsidized (% I don't know) and yes it seems like the only plan is to get people addicted to it and then jack up prices. I don't think it will work as well as needed to generate money BUT I have been disappointed by the typical consumer's habits.

>>61974457
>Hire under paid and under skilled wagie
>Generate tons of AI slop trash advertising
>Here's your marketing department

AI is genuinely going to ruin the quality of so many things in the interest of convenience of cost cutting. Exporting all the jobs to the third world was bad enough.

61975382
>>61975152
> build a product
> subsidize it until it gets popular enough to not require subsidies
> profit
All kinds of businesses operate like this. Sometimes it takes over a decade. People are dooming like ai is the first company to ever not turn a profit in year 1

61975520
>>61975382
Sure, I get it that subsidizing the consumers, while the company is in early stag,e is commonly done by companies, no issues there.
But I don't understand how this would make sense when talking about AI companies.
If they get more customers, that use more tokens, they are still operating at a loss. They would need to make some sort of breakthrough or change, that would let them operate at similar levels, but with lower costs.
Like if I buy Claude subscription for 20$ and my real use cost is more like 50$, if Claude gets 10 million more customers like me, does that help them make profit? They scaled up massively, but they are still losing money.
I don't have a doomer attitude towards AI, but I genuinely don't understand how their current company model is sustainable, I think that there would need to be some sort of major change (which for sure could be just come technological leap) for this companies to actually become profitable.

>>61975352
Yeah I don't know percentages myself, but going by my own usage and other people I interact with, it seems like pretty much most paying customers end up using more than what they pay for and then you have all of the free user's as well.

61975562
>>61973654
>those error bars
>50% of the time
I'm glad I'm not in on this shit. I'd be a nervous wreck watching for the top constantly

61975598
>>61975520
Another problem is all LLM corps are competing for the same customer base. I don't know the theoretical market of people who need AI, but they'll all gravitate to the one that's the best. Everyone else who dumped tens of billions cannot possibly recover costs. I also wonder what a fair market adjusted AI sub would be, no subsides and intended for profit. There's just no way they make this money back.

61975600
>>61975520
Maybe they adopt the (doomed) gym membership strategy:
>work on scaling up consumer base
>lower subscription fees to entice new customers
>expect most of your subscription base to rarely/never use their token share
With a brick and mortar, this works due to peoples' inherent inertia, but I doubt it could work where the subscription is accessible to bed rotters (not that they wouldn't try. greed is a hell of a thing)

61975687
>>61975598
Good point.

>>61975600
I thought of this as well, so that you would essentially have part of your customers effectively subsidizing your other customers, but I don't think it would work for AI companies. Most people would probably get a monthly plan and if they wouldn't use it, they wouldn't renew it. Like you mentioned yourself, I don't think this would work with such online service subscriptions.

61975692
>>61975520
Costs are coming down, in some cases by as much as 100x a year. A lot of those gains are then fed back into making smarter ai, but not all of it

61975715
For example, you can now get a 9B sized LLM that’s just as smart as GPT5-OSS from fall last year, which was I think 120B. Improvements are coming fast rn

61975928
>>61975715
That's great, how are businesses and consumers going to use these smarter LLMs? Pure AI companies make the absolute least amount of money and are the most desperate to, how many times over do their revenues need to multiply before they hit bare bones profitability?

>>61975600
That would be a fitting end for AI, the manmade god, ends up working like Planet Fitness.

61975989
>>61975928
If you can’t understand how “does 4 hours of work in 2 minutes” changes the world that’s a failure on your end. We’re already there and it’s only getting better.

Btw here’s a new post about efficiency, 1000x in one year lmao
https://x.com/chatgpt21/status/2031865810570862783

61976065
>>61975989
You must have had chat summarize the thread. I never argue that AI tech isn't sticking around, only that it is currently way overvalued and infrastructure is many years away. It will correct.

This is the problem with people using AI to think for them.

Every post you've shared is AI generated X slop, I get what you're saying but these are garbage.

61976110
>>61976065
> show me some evidence
> does so
> uhhhh that doesn’t count

61976289
>>61975692
I have read about costs coming down, but generally speaking despite that, most people are still using this AI tools at subsidized prices right? How probable is it for the costs to be at some point so discounted that this companies will be actually able to make a profit? And obviously I was focusing mainly just on token costs, there are other costs that this companies face as well.

>>61954004

61954034

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61964509
>>61954140
Isntrael said jump goy jump and we jumped. There was no planing.

61964526
>>61955785
This and also support from brainwashed christcuck boomers. I'm not team remove taco even if he is a jewish puppet

61964533
>>61964526
>not
on

61964536
>>61964526
What the hell? Even the Sons Of Anarchy are evangelicucks?

61964540
>>61961091
How is he wrong?
Fuck pisslam

61964600
>>61954004
americans are the same people who cheered on the invasion of iraq thinking they were getting revenge for 9/11
their demographics and mental/physical health are far worse today than 2 decades ago so i don't understand why so many anons are disappointed at them, as if you hold goycattle to high standards

61964812
>>61954140
The plan is for ISRAEL to dominate the world.
Escalation is necessary and so is mutt blood.

61969335
>>61954140
The thing is, the scenario of Iranians closing down the strait the moment they're backed into a corner has been talked about since forever. Though I suppose after a few major escalations (2020, 12-day war), of which both of them were done under Trump's orders, he thought that Iran wouldn't dare, that they don't have the capability to do such a thing.

61969384
>>61955810
that jak meme is infinitely funnier than your shitty magic the gathering meme

61969494
>>61956133
>Libtards controlled and censored all of social media until Elon bought Twitter.
And now Elon does the same with any opinion that disagrees with him, left or right wing. Just accept that you are a retard.

61971493
>>61954004
Infuriating

61971579
>>61964540
what you're saying is praise judaism

61971592
>>61954140
Iran are not arabs/moors. They have high IQ and know how to deal with the Great Satan with intelligence.
In other words, FAFO

61971605
>>61954039
>>61957437
Umm guys do you know what NATO is? NATO is that thing that forces "eurochads" to join US in every Middle Eastern suicide pact. Did you forget Iraq?

61971620
>>61969335
Iran didn't dare in 2025 but you can only fake peace negotiations while killing everyone doing the negotiations so many times (twice, turns out)

61971631
>>61971605
NATO is totally broken now, burger. And if a european leader even suggested to go die for jews with the US, the ""far"" right would win the elections with a super-majority. They are dumb, but not as dumb as burgers.

61971810
>>61956993
Basically overseas expats should identify as Canadian

61971850
>>61954970
Post image of your keyboard right now.
>It's the Hebrew/English variant

61971852
>>61955310
This.

61971874
>>61955310
Hell is being caught between both parties bullshit

61971906
>>61954970
>geg
Too obvious

61971921
>>61956866
They're just concealing their pedophilia. Dismiss them

61971976
>>61958149
Literally, figuratively, unironically yes, that is precisely what is happening. With Larry Ellison buying ALL (no that is not an exaggeration) of the legacy media (CBS, CNN, HBO, etc.) on behalf of Israel and the massive Israeli disinfo bots on Reddit, here, X, TikTok etc. The absolute lowest common denominator, bottom of the barrel normie legitimately believes "America is winning! War is over1!!!1!!". You have to be, as the YNs say, "Tapped In" in order to know how dire the situation reallly is. The Jewish disinfo bots aren't as bad here on /biz/ as they are on /pol/ and /b/ but they're here. They're the ones spewing bullshit like what you replied to. Luckily the veil keeps lifting as the days go by and the bots can't drown out ALL of the truthful posts from social media, so it becomes quite easy to become "Tapped In.". When the entirety of the media tells you we are winning, but then you go to the gas station for your daily fill-up of your lifted F-350 Super Duty with the double cabin that you bought at 27% APR for 96 months and see the 90 cent overnight price increase, you start to question whether we really are "winning" and that leads you to Google which leads you to the current news of how an American oil tanker was just shot by a missile, very clearly contradicting the "We are winning." narrative. Even the most retarded conservative boomer who bought that truck at those horrible rates can still add 1+1.

61971989
>>61971976
>YNs
what

61972961
>>61954004
Oh look, he saved caps lock for the part where he insults Americans again, instead of just turning it on for the entire post. Thats nice. Fuckin boomers scum.

61973140
>>61971631
Anon, the far right are statistically the biggest Israel glazers on earth.
Just look at Millei, Orban, Fico, MAGA, and the rest of the clowns. """"Conservatives"""" have been insisting that sucking off Bibi is the real trad way for ages and they're still at it.

61973149
>>61971989
young niggas

61973750
>>61956133
> you are not allowed to post lies
> posts lies a lot
> gets banned
NOOOOOOO IM BEING CENSORED FOR MY POLITICAL OPINIONS REEEEEEEEEEEEEE

61974076
>>61954918
>I just can't understand
No shit

On another note I found a 10k gold ring in my kitchen today.

61974185
>>61954918
They’ve been brainwashed ever since the mid 90s with angry man talk radio. Then Fox News. Then the boomer internet. 3 of my family members fell victim to that scam, one of them even became a schizo mountain hermit because of it.

OP

look at 1 day chart for WTI futures.

too lazy to post image but, something is going to happen very soon. likely it will dump though. it's been "flat" for too long.
